features.name: AgentAdapter Protocol
features.description: Define an AgentAdapter protocol in dkmv/adapters/base.py that abstracts all agent-specific behavior: command construction, stream parsing, auth configuration, instruction file conventions, Docker requirements, and capability declarations (resume, budget, max_turns).

features.name: ClaudeCodeAdapter Extraction
features.description: Extract existing Claude Code logic from sandbox.py (command construction), stream.py (event parsing), component.py (auth/credentials), and runner.py (instructions path) into a ClaudeCodeAdapter that produces byte-identical behavior. stream_claude() becomes a thin wrapper preserving existing method signatures for 30+ test mocks.

features.name: CodexCLIAdapter Implementation
features.description: Implement a CodexCLIAdapter supporting: codex exec command construction with --json --full-auto --sandbox danger-full-access --skip-git-repo-check, JSONL stream parsing (dot-separated event types: thread.started, turn.completed, item.started/completed), AGENTS.md instruction file convention, CODEX_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY auth, and session resume via codex exec resume <session_id>.

features.name: Agent Resolution Cascade
features.description: Implement 7-level agent resolution: task YAML agent field > manifest task_ref agent > component manifest agent > CLI --agent flag > project config defaults.agent > DKMVConfig default_agent > built-in default 'claude'. Add agent field to TaskDefinition, ManifestTaskRef, ComponentManifest, BaseComponentConfig, CLIOverrides, and ProjectDefaults.

features.name: Agent-Model Validation and Inference
features.description: Each adapter validates model compatibility (claude-* for Claude, gpt-*/o-series for Codex). When model is set without explicit agent, infer agent from model prefix via infer_agent_from_model(). Auto-substitute agent's default model when resolved model is from wrong family (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-6 with agent=codex becomes gpt-5.3-codex with info log).

features.name: Multi-Agent Docker Image
features.description: Update Dockerfile to install both Claude Code (@anthropic-ai/claude-code) and Codex CLI (@openai/codex) with version pinning via build args. Pre-configure Codex with ~/.codex/config.toml for headless Docker usage (sandbox mode, network access). Add --codex-version flag to dkmv build command.

features.name: Codex Credential Discovery in Init
features.description: Extend dkmv init credential discovery step to detect CODEX_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY. Add auth method options: Claude-only, Codex-only, or Both. Store codex_api_key_source in CredentialSources. Non-interactive --yes mode auto-detects available credentials.

features.name: Mixed-Agent Components
features.description: Support components where different tasks use different agents (e.g., plan with Claude, implement with Codex). _build_sandbox_config() scans all task refs before the task loop to determine agents_needed and passes credentials for ALL agents into the container. Adapter instantiated per-task, not per-component.

features.name: Stream Event Normalization
features.description: StreamParser gains optional adapter parameter for delegated parsing. Codex JSONL events (thread.started, turn.completed, item.started/completed with snake_case item types) are normalized to existing StreamEvent dataclass. Codex adapter accumulates turn counts and token usage across turn.completed events, emitting a single result StreamEvent on thread.closed.

features.name: CLI Agent Selection
features.description: Add --agent option to all 5 run commands (dev, qa, docs, plan, run_component) and add agent field to CLIOverrides. Agent flag participates in the resolution cascade at priority level 4.

personas.name: Claude Code Developer
personas.description: Existing DKMV user who uses Claude Code exclusively. Must experience zero behavioral changes — identical CLI commands, stream parsing, auth flow. All existing YAML files, configs, and CLI invocations work without modification.

personas.name: Codex CLI Developer
personas.description: Developer who prefers OpenAI's Codex CLI for coding tasks. Wants to use dkmv dev --agent codex or set defaults.agent=codex in project config. May use CODEX_API_KEY or existing OPENAI_API_KEY.

personas.name: Multi-Agent Developer
personas.description: Advanced user who leverages both agents within a single component — e.g., planning with Claude (stronger reasoning) and implementing with Codex (cost optimization via subscription). Configures agent at the task-ref level in component manifests.

personas.name: CI/CD Pipeline Operator
personas.description: Sets up DKMV in CI environments with env var credentials. Needs dkmv init --yes to auto-detect available credentials (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CODEX_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY) and configure accordingly. Builds Docker images with both agents via dkmv build.

constraints: Zero breaking changes: all existing YAML files, CLI commands, and configs must work without modification
All 720+ existing tests must pass without modification after Phase 1 refactor
Coverage >= 80% overall, >= 90% for new adapter code
Quality gates: ruff check, ruff format, mypy, pytest must all pass per phase
Claude adapter must produce byte-identical CLI commands to current hardcoded implementation
stream_claude() on SandboxManager must preserve exact current signature so 30+ test mocks continue working
Config version stays at 1; new fields must have defaults for backwards compatibility
Single Docker image for both agents (separate images deferred per NG1)
Python 3.12+; use str | None not Optional[str], list[str] not List[str]
Conventional commits with scopes: init, project, registry, config, cli, sandbox, rename, adapter

risks.risk: R1: Claude Code behavioral regression — refactoring stream_claude() into adapter pattern introduces subtle differences
risks.mitigation: Phase 1 is pure refactor; Claude adapter must produce byte-identical commands. stream_claude() kept as thin wrapper. Add regression test asserting exact command string. All 720+ tests must pass unmodified.

risks.risk: R2: Stream event parsing drift — normalizing Codex events may lose information or produce unexpected StreamEvents
risks.mitigation: StreamEvent.raw always preserves original event dict. render_event() only uses normalized fields. Add snapshot tests comparing parsed Codex events to expected StreamEvent values.

risks.risk: R3: Auth credential leakage — CODEX_API_KEY could leak via logs or error messages
risks.mitigation: Same masking as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Pass via Docker -e flag, never log. shlex.quote() all values. Never include API keys in JSONL or run artifacts.

risks.risk: R4: Docker image size bloat from adding Codex CLI
risks.mitigation: Codex CLI is ~50-100MB (Rust binary). Track image size in CI with 5GB threshold. Separate images (NG1) available as future escape hatch.

risks.risk: R5: Codex sandbox conflicts inside Docker — Landlock/Seatbelt may not work in containers
risks.mitigation: Use --sandbox danger-full-access to disable Codex's OS-level sandbox. Docker provides isolation. Test explicitly in integration tests.

risks.risk: R6: Codex CLI JSONL format changes between versions
risks.mitigation: Pin Codex version in Dockerfile (same pattern as Claude Code). Adapter's parse_event() is defensive — unknown events become StreamEvent(type=event_type, raw=data). Snapshot tests for known event types.

risks.risk: R7: Mixed-agent credential handling — component needs both Claude and Codex credentials in same container
risks.mitigation: All credentials passed as env vars at container start. _build_sandbox_config() scans agents_needed from manifest and includes all required credentials. Adapters only reference their own env vars in build_command().
adapter.validate_model() returns False; DKMV prints clear error and exits before container startup (fast fail).

risks.risk: R9: Codex resume unreliable in container environment
risks.mitigation: supports_resume() defaults True (--ephemeral omitted). If testing reveals issues, flip to False and retry logic re-runs from scratch.

risks.risk: R10: Codex config file conflicts from cloned repos (.codex/config.toml)
risks.mitigation: Pre-write DKMV-controlled config in Dockerfile at ~/.codex/config.toml. Investigate --config flag or env var override during Phase 3.

non_goals: NG1: Separate Docker images per agent — deferred; single image is simpler for v1
NG2: Codex Cloud (remote execution) support — runs on OpenAI infrastructure, not in DKMV containers
NG3: Local model support (Ollama via Codex --oss) — out of scope; can be added later via adapter
NG4: Agent-specific MCP server configuration — complex; deferred
NG5: Changing the .agent/ directory convention inside containers — ADR-0010 stands
NG6: Auto-migration of existing .dkmv/config.json files — version stays at 1; new fields have defaults
NG7: Changes to SWE-ReX integration or container lifecycle — adapter only affects command/stream/instructions
NG8: Per-agent custom built-in components — built-in components remain agent-agnostic
NG9: Changing hardcoded model names in built-in component YAMLs — model defaults overridden by --model/--agent at runtime
architecture_notes: Adapter Pattern: AgentAdapter as Python Protocol (structural typing) with concrete implementations per agent. Adapter is lightweight, stateless beyond configuration.
Adapter Lifecycle: Instantiated per-task (not per-component) because different tasks may use different agents within a mixed-agent component.
Resolution Cascade (7 levels): task YAML > task_ref > manifest > CLI --agent > project config > DKMVConfig > built-in default 'claude'. Mirrors existing model resolution for consistency.
Backwards Compatibility: stream_claude() on SandboxManager kept with exact current signature, internally creates ClaudeCodeAdapter and delegates to stream_agent(). This preserves 30+ test mocks.
Credential Scanning: Before task loop, ComponentRunner scans all task refs to determine agents_needed set, passes ALL required credentials to _build_sandbox_config().
Stream Normalization: All agents produce the same StreamEvent dataclass. Codex adapter accumulates turn counts across turn.completed events and emits single result on thread.closed.
4-phase implementation: Phase 1 (pure refactor, zero behavior change), Phase 2 (Codex adapter + agent selection), Phase 3 (Docker + init integration), Phase 4 (polish + docs).

11 coupling points identified and verified against actual codebase: CP-1 through CP-11 covering command construction, stream parsing, instructions file, auth, config, Dockerfile, .gitignore, error messages, build command, and default model.
adrs_reviewed: ADR-0002: Claude Code Only for v1 — this PRD supersedes it by introducing multi-agent support. ADR-0002 explicitly planned for v2 extensibility via the adapter pattern now being implemented.

ADR-0007: Config via Environment Variables Only — unchanged. New fields (CODEX_API_KEY, DKMV_AGENT) follow the same env var pattern via pydantic-settings. Project config (ADR-0009) cascade also unchanged.
ADR-0009: Project-scoped Configuration — additive changes only. New fields (codex_api_key_source, defaults.agent) have defaults. Config version stays at 1.
ADR-0012: YAML Manifest Component System — additive agent field to ComponentManifest and ManifestTaskRef. No changes to YAML loading, variable system, or task execution model.

alternatives_considered: Duck typing with no formal interface
technology_decisions.rationale: Protocol enables structural subtyping — adapters don't need to inherit from a base class. This matches the existing codebase pattern (no deep inheritance hierarchies). Protocols are checked by mypy at type-check time, providing safety without runtime overhead. ABC would work but forces inheritance and is more rigid.

v0.110.0 is the current latest (March 2026), actively maintained with frequent releases. Pin to specific version in Dockerfile (same pattern as Claude Code @2.1.47) for reproducibility. The --json JSONL streaming, --full-auto, --sandbox danger-full-access, and exec resume features are all confirmed available. Default model is gpt-5.3-codex.

technology_decisions.chosen: CODEX_API_KEY env var with OPENAI_API_KEY fallback
alternatives_considered: Device auth (codex login --device-auth) with bind-mounted auth.json
technology_decisions.rationale: CODEX_API_KEY is the documented CI/non-interactive approach. OPENAI_API_KEY fallback covers the common case where users already have this set. ChatGPT OAuth requires a browser (impractical in Docker). Device auth is a viable future enhancement but adds complexity for v1.

technology_decisions.chosen: Prepend DKMV system context to workspace AGENTS.md
technology_decisions.rationale: Prepending preserves project-specific instructions from cloned repos while adding DKMV's agent context. This mirrors the Claude Code approach where CLAUDE.md can have project-level instructions alongside DKMV's. AGENTS.override.md could work but is semantically different (per-directory override, not additive).

--full-auto provides auto-approval semantics. --sandbox danger-full-access overrides the default workspace-write sandbox since Docker already provides isolation and Codex's OS-level sandbox (Landlock/seccomp) may not be available inside Docker. Avoids --yolo which disables safety features we may want to keep.

questions.question: How should the adapter interface be implemented?

options.label: Python Protocol (structural typing)
options.description: Adapters satisfy the interface structurally — no inheritance required. Checked by mypy. Lightweight and matches existing codebase patterns. Cannot provide default implementations.

options.label: Abstract Base Class
options.description: Adapters inherit from AgentAdapter ABC. Can provide default implementations for common methods. More explicit but forces inheritance hierarchy.

options.label: Protocol + concrete base class
options.description: Protocol defines the interface for type checking. A concrete BaseAdapter class provides shared defaults (e.g., build_command suffix with redirect/background). Adapters extend BaseAdapter and satisfy Protocol.

questions.question: Should Phase 1 create a new ADR to formally supersede ADR-0002 (Claude Code Only)?

options.label: Create ADR-0016
options.description: Write a formal ADR-0016 documenting the multi-agent adapter architecture decision. Mark ADR-0002 as superseded. Provides clear architectural record.

options.label: Update ADR-0002 status
options.description: Change ADR-0002's status to 'Superseded by multi-agent adapter (PRD v1.0)' with a brief note. No new ADR file — the PRD itself serves as the decision document.

questions.question: How aggressively should Codex version be pinned?

options.label: Exact version (0.110.0)
options.description: Pin to exact version in Dockerfile ARG. Maximum reproducibility. Requires manual bumps to get new features/fixes.

options.label: Default to 'latest', pin in CI
options.description: Dockerfile ARG defaults to 'latest' but CI/production builds specify exact version. Easier local dev, controlled production builds.

options.label: Semver range (^0.110.0)
options.description: Allow compatible updates. npm resolves to latest 0.x. Balances updates with stability, but pre-1.0 semver is less predictable.

questions.question: Should the implementation plan follow the PRD's 4-phase structure or be reorganized?

options.label: Follow PRD phases exactly
options.description: Phase 1: Adapter Foundation (refactor), Phase 2: Codex + Agent Selection, Phase 3: Docker + Init, Phase 4: Polish. Matches the PRD's risk-ordered approach — refactor first, then add.

options.label: Merge into 3 phases
options.description: Merge Docker/Init (Phase 3) into Phase 2 since they're closely coupled with Codex adapter work. Fewer integration points to manage. Phase 1: Refactor, Phase 2: Codex (adapter + Docker + init + agent selection), Phase 3: Polish.

options.label: Vertical slices by feature
options.description: Organize by complete features rather than technical layers. Slice 1: Full Claude adapter extraction + tests. Slice 2: Full Codex adapter + Docker + init + tests. Slice 3: Agent selection cascade + CLI + mixed-agent. Each slice is independently deployable.
